---
title: "About Math Nomad"
page-layout: full
toc: false
body-classes: mn-about-page
---

::: {.content-block .mn-about}
::: {.mn-about-main}
## A travelling workshop for mathematical ideas

Math Nomad is an independent project by **Mohan Rajendran**, a mathematician and educator at Azim Premji University. It brings together course materials, research-informed exposition, outreach, videos and interactive projects.

The project is built around a simple principle: a mathematical idea becomes more generous when readers can meet it in several ways. They may manipulate an example, read an argument, take a problem to a classroom, or inspect the computation behind a finite claim.

The intended audience includes curious members of the public, school teachers, undergraduate and graduate students, researchers, problem solvers and anyone who likes mathematics enough to pause over a pattern.
:::

::: {.mn-about-aside}
### What you will find here

- curated course pathways through notes, exercises, interactives and useful links;
- writing at several levels, from public exposition to research ideas;
- video companions with references and extended descriptions;
- active projects with visible progress and related outputs;
- reusable classroom investigations and teacher notes.
:::
:::

::: {.mn-principles-band}
::: {.content-block}
## Editorial principles

::: {.mn-three-up}
::: {.mn-path-card}
### Invite first

Begin with a question, object or phenomenon that gives the reader something meaningful to do.
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
### Explain honestly

Distinguish experiment, computation, conjecture and proof. Each has a distinct and valuable role.
:::

::: {.mn-path-card}
### Make it reusable

Pair an interactive with enough mathematical and pedagogical context for another person to use it well.
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.content-block .mn-about-publishing}
::: {.mn-two-up}
::: {}
<p class="mn-kicker">HOW THE SITE FITS</p>

## A companion to formal publication

Journals and institutional venues remain the formal record for work published there. Math Nomad complements that record with material for which those formats rarely have room: interactives, code, catalogues, classroom adaptations, computational appendices and behind-the-paper notes.

When a piece is connected to a journal publication, the version shared here will comply with that venue’s copyright and self-archiving policies. The aim is to extend the work, not to duplicate a version whose distribution is restricted.
:::

::: {}
<p class="mn-kicker">FOLLOW AT A HUMAN PACE</p>

## Return when something is ready

Math Nomad is intended to grow steadily, without turning publication into a treadmill. [The main RSS feed](index.xml) carries every new publication. A low-frequency email digest, bringing together the strongest recent work three or four times a year, is planned.
:::
:::
:::
